query: Wikimedia Foundation Board Trustees Wikinews closure May 2026

{"query":"Wikimedia Foundation Board Trustees Wikinews closure May 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1768,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikinews.org/wiki/Wikimedia_Foundation_closes_Wikinews_after_21_years","title":"Wikimedia Foundation closes Wikinews after 21 years - Wikinews, the free news source","content":"The Board of Trustees of the Wikimedia Foundation announced on March 30 its decision to close all language editions of the project.
After this process, the Board announced in March that the 31 active editions of Wikinews would be placed in read-only mode indefinitely.
Following the launch of the English and German editions in December 2004, additional language editions were created in early 2005, including Dutch, French, Swedish, Spanish, Bulgarian, Polish, Romanian, and Portuguese.
At the time that the closure of Wikinews was announced, there were reportedly about 700 active editors among all 31 language editions combined.

The mission of the Wikimedia Foundation is to empower and engage people worldwide to collect and develop educational content under a free license or in the public domain, and to disseminate it effectively and globally.

In July 2021, another bulk import of articles into Russian Wikinews led to a similar overloading of certain systems, which shut down access to all Wikis for 30 minutes, and a permanent disabling of DPL.
All sysops in each Wikinews wiki will be removed.

Starting on May 4, editing and new content creation will no longer be possible with all of the pages on the site locked in read-only mode.
The Italian version of Wikinews has reported that the WMF will issue a public statement on the project's closure on April 4, likely to elaborate more on the technical transition to read-only mode and the preservation of existing content, as anticipated by Doronina in her own post.

This topic was discussed at","score":0.9435197,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikinews.org/wiki/Wikinews:Closure_of_Wikinews","title":"Wikinews:Closure of Wikinews - Wikinews, the free news source","content":"**The Wikimedia Foundation's Board of Trustees has decided to close all Wikinews projects.** From 4 May, Wikinews sites will become read-only. You are probably visiting this page from the sitenotices. On this page you will find information about how and when the closure process took place, along with other details related to the closing of Wikinews. * This was followed by a clarification from the Sister Projects Task Force (SPTF), which had been given the task of conducting a public consultation about Wikinews and submitting their final analysis to the Board of Trustees. * **Late June 2025:** A public consultation was opened to gather opinions and comments from the wider Wikimedia community. * **Late November 2025:** The SPTF released the results of the public consultation along with their formal recommendation to the Board of Trustees. All Wikinews language editions will be read-only from May 4, 2026. The future of Wikinews is unclear as of May 2026.","score":0.914176,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikinews.org/wiki/Wikimedia_Foundation_closes_Wikinews_after_21_years","title":"Wikimedia Foundation closes Wikinews after 21 years - Wikinews, the free news source","content":"**The Wikimedia Foundation's Board of Trustees has decided to close all Wikinews projects.** From 4 May, Wikinews sites will become read-only. | This article mentions the Wikimedia Foundation, one of its projects, or people related to it. *Wikinews* was a project of the Wikimedia Foundation. Logo of Wikinews, adopted by the community through a logo contest held in January–February 2005. The Board of Trustees of the Wikimedia Foundation announced on March 30 its decision to close all language editions of the project. The announcement stated that all *Wikinews* editions—referring to the multiple language sections of *Wikinews*—would \"transition to read-only mode\" on May 4. And I think that is providing some kind of a tension in terms of getting Wikipedians to write for an organization such as Wikinews.\" In 2013, a link to *Wikinews* was removed from the \"In the News\" section of the front page of Wikipedia, and links to *Wikinews* were also removed from the Current Events portal in that year.","score":0.8593928,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Wikipedia_Signpost/2026-03-31/News_and_notes","title":"Wikipedia:Wikipedia Signpost/2026-03-31/News and notes - Wikipedia","content":"< Wikipedia:Wikipedia Signpost | 2026-03-31. Entirety of Wikinews to be shut down: All languages to be shut down in May; first AI agent blocked; new name for AfD? On March 30, Board of Trustees member Victoria Doronina confirmed in a mailing list post that the Foundation has decided to permanently shut down the Wikinews project, one of Wikimedia's oldest projects. The Italian version of Wikinews has reported that the WMF will issue a public statement on the project's closure on April 4, likely to elaborate more on the technical transition to read-only mode and the preservation of existing content, as anticipated by Doronina in her own post. However, the project has always struggled to gain momentum in comparison to other Wikimedia portals throughout the years: at the time of this issue's publication, the platform is active in 31 languages, with just over 700 active editors across the board.","score":0.85825616,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikinews","title":"Wikinews - Wikipedia","content":"# Wikinews. From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia. This article is about the project of the Wikimedia Foundation; it is not to be confused with the general concept of Wiki journalism. **Wikinews** is a discontinued free-content news wiki and a project of the Wikimedia Foundation that gathered and reported news collaboratively through user-created content. Wikipedia co-founder Jimmy Wales has distinguished Wikinews from Wikipedia by saying, \"On Wikinews, each story is to be written as a news story as opposed to an encyclopedia article.\". In March 2026, the WMF announced that Wikinews would be closed and made read-only on May 4 of that year. The first recorded proposal of a Wikimedia news site was a two-line anonymous post on January 5, 2003, on the Wikimedia community's Meta-Wiki. As of May 2026, Wikinews content pages have been created in 36 editions, all of which were made read-only on the week of May 4 of that year.","score":0.8095324,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.91,"request_id":"529b3995-db66-4b78-b654-c888f58cbaea"}
